reason: Core reference for CKA layer-pairing and representation similarity diagnostics.
CCA-based representation comparison and possible layer-pairing ablation.
Projection-weighted CCA alternative for comparing activation spaces.
Random orthogonal transforms as variance-reduced structured projections.
Hadamard-based fast structured random transforms.
Fourier mixing reference for cheap dense transforms.
Learnable FFT-like structured transforms for rotation-family ablations.
Rotation plus product quantization as a direct compression analogue.
Hadamard rotations for outlier-free LLM quantization.
Incoherence processing and trellis quantization for low-bit LLM compression.
Randomized low-rank linear algebra for reduced-rank alignment ablations.
Activation outlier smoothing and per-channel scaling for quantization ablations.
Affine transformation based post-training quantization for rotation/scale ablations.
Kalman-style latent-state estimation and confidence-weighted measurement fusion.
Confidence-based adaptive routing and compute allocation.
Shrinkage-style blending when translated KV is noisy.
Cross-layer singular-vector alignment for KV-cache compression.
Head-weighting and dynamic head selection instead of flat concatenation.
Causal head diagnostics for deciding which heads to transmit.
Saliency-guided KV compression and token selection.
Hadamard rotation plus linear correction for low-bit KV repair.
Outlier-aware adaptive rotations and grouped-head quantization.
Asymmetric key/value quantization baseline for the fused KV path.
Low-rank projection as a compact KV representation baseline.
Trainable orthogonal projection for adaptive KV compression.
Source coding with decoder side information as a direct communication analogue.
Adaptive gating and routing for token-level compute allocation.
Event-triggered communication based on confidence thresholds.
Explicit rate-distortion framing for accuracy-versus-bytes sweeps.
Residual-error transmission and iterative correction as a KV-comms analogue.
Model stitching evidence that transferable linear features exist across language models.
Theory grounding for when linear or near-linear cross-model alignment should succeed.
Spectral compression baseline for testing whether transformed latent signals are easier to transmit.
Exploratory multiresolution-attention reference for future frequency-aware head selection and routing.
Layer-wise asymmetric KV quantization evidence that keys and values should not share the same compression policy.
Retrieval-head view of long-context inference; motivates selective K-only head retention.
Head-level KV cache retention directly supports the grouped-head and K-only branches.
Key-token selection evidence for position-sparse K-only transport.
Per-head position selection for KV retention; useful for token-sparse communication ablations.
Heavy-hitter token retention framing for selective cache transmission.
KV-centric benchmark framing for evaluating communication and compression claims more directly.
Task-aware adaptive KV budgets support content-dependent communication schedules.
Reasoning-specific redundancy analysis for KV compression; especially relevant for GSM8K-style tasks.
Direct support for asymmetric treatment of keys and values, matching the emerging K-only result.
Evidence that keys and values behave differently across local and global cache structure, supporting the K-only branch.
Attention-fidelity objective for preserving QK structure rather than only raw KV reconstruction.
Periodic lossless retention suggests structured position-level sparsity instead of uniform transmission.
Direct token-level KV selection method for the next sparse-key transport ablations.
Primary query-aware sparsity reference for selective key-position retention instead of uniform transport.
Reliability-weighted KV fusion baseline that can inform source-dependent blending instead of one fixed gate.
Supports attention-space preservation objectives when KV reconstruction alone is not predictive of downstream quality.
Query-centric cache fusion is a direct architectural reference for source-conditioned fusion beyond scalar gate sweeps.
Adds a query-aware token-retention baseline where live self-attention decides which KV positions survive.
Direct motivation for task-aware head and selector ablations instead of one universal sparse policy.
Provides a stronger query-blind compression baseline to compare against live query-aware sparse import.
Mechanistic evidence that only certain heads matter for retrieval, supporting retrieval-head-only sparse transport.
Supports query-focused retrieval-head selection and attention-logit-preserving routing for reasoning tasks.
Adds a simple norm-based sparse-selector baseline so we can compare complex query-aware routing against a cheap heuristic.
Provides a future-query expected-attention prior for stronger fixed selector baselines beyond last-token attention.
Direct QK-geometry reference for attention-logit-preserving sparse selection and selector scoring.
Motivates variable per-head budgets instead of a flat head-selection ratio across the bridge.
Supports offline head reordering and calibration-guided retrieval-head transport as the next mechanistic ablation.
Motivates task-adaptive or query-adaptive per-head budget schedules instead of one fixed runtime head ratio.
Direct sparse principal-key reference for retrieval-head concentration and top-key transport ablations.
Provides a mechanistic localization route for future causal head scoring instead of purely similarity-based or attention-based selection.
Head-wise offloading turns heads into an explicit systems budget axis for sparse transport ablations.
Supports fine-grained query-aware retrieval and routing beyond coarse position-only selection.
Reinforces the current K-only direction by arguing keys deserve more budget than values.
Hierarchical KV sharing motivates multi-level routing across layers, heads, and queries.
Head-specific retention is a direct reference for retrieval-head-only or calibration-mask transport.
Adaptive budget allocation motivates variable per-head budgets instead of one flat keep ratio.
Head-level key pruning is directly relevant to calibrated head-mask and head-pruning ablations.
Layer-wise dynamic budget allocation is a direct next-step comparator for fixed calibration-derived head budgets.
Classic evidence that attention-head utility is sharply nonuniform, supporting head-prior and retrieval-head stories.
Provides the cautionary framing needed if fixed head priors outperform live attention-derived routing.
Adaptive probabilistic retention is a stronger query-blind baseline for sparse-key routing and budget allocation.
Dynamic heterogeneous group attention offers a stronger learned alternative to one flat per-head budget rule.
Search-based KV budget optimization is a useful contrast to simple fixed-prior or live-attention routing.
Retrieval-vs-streaming head separation is a direct inspiration for fixed head-prior budgeting and head-type transfer tests.
Attention sinks provide a strong blind prior and failure-boundary reference for streaming or sink-token retention controls.
Entropy-aware head scoring is a direct next-step feature for making calibrated head priors more stable than plain attention peaks.
Causal head scoring is a reviewer-facing mechanism check for whether the winning sparse priors actually target causally useful heads.
Low-rank attention geometry is a direct reference for factorizing or shrinking the head-prior tensor instead of using raw per-head scores.
Mechanistic retrieval-head evidence strengthens the claim that sparse key import is acting through a retrieval-style subcircuit rather than generic cache perturbation.
Nonlinear shrinkage gives a mathematically grounded way to stabilize noisy per-head covariance and prior estimates before transfer.
Rate-distortion theory is the cleanest formal framing for sparse head-budget allocation and accuracy-vs-bytes curves.
Semantic retrieval heads directly support the current paper direction that a small retrieval-oriented head subset determines which key positions are worth transporting.
Layer-wise dissimilar KV sharing is a close systems analogue for pair-conditioned cross-model cache reuse and a useful comparison point for asymmetric transfer.
Length-aware attention priors are a direct reference for using cached priors to bias test-time reasoning under a fixed inference budget.
Query-focused retrieval heads are a direct mechanism reference for the live query-aware sparse routing branch and for retrieval-head-only ablations.
Dynamic retrieval heads directly support the current concern that static fixed priors may be brittle across queries and seeds.
Attention-logit interpolation is a direct reference for preserving the useful attention geometry rather than only preserving raw head-score magnitudes.
Learnable permutation is a direct symmetry-aware reference for reordering heads or channels before sparse routing or cross-model matching.
Query-focused memory-aware reranking is a strong live query-conditioned comparator for sparse key routing and selector stability.
Cross-task and cross-lingual head contribution analysis supports the claim that head importance can shift systematically across settings, complicating static prior reuse.
Gauge symmetry gives a direct mathematical explanation for why fixed head identities can be unstable across seeds and model pairs, motivating explicit head matching before routing.
Matching key/query distributions is a direct reference for attention-logit-preserving head ranking and QK-geometry-aware alignment.
Q-Filters is the clearest direct reference for using QK geometry, rather than raw cache statistics, to preserve useful sparse attention structure.
Graph optimal transport supplies a principled cross-space matching objective for aligning head or layer structure across different model geometries.
Linear covariance shrinkage is a lightweight statistical template for stabilizing noisy head-prior estimates across calibration seeds.
Expected-attention priors are a stronger query-blind comparator for sparse routing and a useful control against overclaiming live query dependence.
Attention-fidelity guarantees are the cleanest direct reference for preserving QK geometry rather than only matching head scores or norms.
Transformer fusion with OT is a direct symmetry-aware baseline for head or layer matching when identities are not canonical across models.
Selective induction heads provide a causal-structure explanation for why useful head sets should vary with context instead of staying fixed.
Wasserstein Procrustes is a canonical unsupervised alignment reference for cross-space matching under permutation and geometry ambiguity.
Quantized Wasserstein Procrustes is a practical lighter-weight OT alignment reference that may fit head or group matching better than full transport on this machine.
SAGE-KV is a strong query-blind sparse-retention baseline for testing whether structured routing really beats self-attention-guided eviction heuristics.
Thin Keys, Full Values is a direct K/V asymmetry reference that matches the current evidence that key transport is more useful than symmetric KV transfer.
Crystal-KV is a direct reference for task-conditioned cache budgeting and answer-first reasoning structure, which may explain the GSM versus SVAMP split.
Low-Rank Key Value Attention is a direct head-subspace geometry reference for why grouped CCA or other shared low-rank bases may help on some reasoning tasks.
Learnable permutation is the closest direct structured-sparsity reference for fixing non-canonical head order before sparse routing.
PermLLM provides a lightweight Sinkhorn-style soft-permutation template for channel or head remapping under sparsity constraints.
Task-KV is a direct reference for task-conditioned head utility, which matches the current GSM versus SVAMP split.
KVLinC is the cleanest lightweight linear-correction reference to pair with matched sparse transport when pure routing heuristics plateau.
Maximal gauge symmetry is the clearest direct mathematical explanation for why head identity can remain non-canonical even after naive matching.
Transport and Merge is a direct OT / transport reference for cross-architecture alignment when component identity is not canonical.
FlashSinkhorn is a practical entropic-OT reference for making soft transport cheap enough to prototype on this machine.
This sharper gauge-symmetry characterization strengthens the current blocker claim that head identity can stay non-canonical even after naive matching or sparse routing.
This is a cleaner direct citation for orthogonal Procrustes as a geometry-preserving alignment primitive before sparse transport.
This gives a principled OT framing for representation evolution and supports moving from heuristic head ranking toward soft transport plans.
This is a practical contextual-embedding OT alignment reference for matching relations and geometry rather than raw coordinates.
This is a recent cross-model KV reuse reference that supports pairing reusable transport maps with lightweight adaptation instead of relying on a fully fixed head-routing heuristic.
This is a direct multi-LoRA KV-sharing reference for separating reusable shared-cache structure from adapter-specific cache deltas rather than treating reuse as all-or-nothing.

This is a recent copy-on-write KV reuse reference that supports splitting stable shared transport from branch-specific cache growth, which matches the current blocker around brittle fully fixed transport maps.
This is a direct reference for why runtime self-correction can matter even when a static upstream transformation looks plausible, which matches the new correction-versus-transport blocker.
This is the cleanest layer-dynamics reference for the hypothesis that runtime layer-to-layer correction matters more than another static transport-only shortcut.
This is a direct residual-KV correction reference that supports trying transport plus a small residual path rather than transport-only or selector-only shortcuts.
This is a learned KV autoencoding and reuse reference, useful for motivating compact learned correction layers on top of transported caches.
This is a direct theory-and-evidence citation for why lightweight cross-model correction should be possible under a platonic/shared-concept representation view.
This is a recent latent-transport reference that motivates uncertainty-aware geometric alignment rather than fixed hard maps when cross-model coordinates are only partially compatible.
This is a direct Gaussian Wasserstein / Bures transport reference for richer covariance-aware costs, useful if the next transport branch moves from heuristic penalties toward principled OT geometry.

This is the cleanest current reference for gauge-aware KV canonicalization, directly supporting the blocker that head-space mismatch is partly a symmetry problem rather than just a weak selector or poor local cost.
This is the most relevant transport-plus-lightweight-correction reference for the remaining positive-method lane after correction-only and weak transport variants have already failed.
